title: ImpersonationEndTrigger enum seed
description: Canonical impersonation-end-trigger taxonomy. Consumed at boot by `ImpersonationEndTrigger` enum registration. Every value maps to a distinct auditability signal — SOC 2 evidence reviewers use end_trigger to segment sessions during review.
columns_note: Fields on each row: `key` (enum member name, snake_case, stable identifier), `display_name` (human-readable label), `sort_order` (picker sort ascending), `is_administrative` (whether this trigger fires from admin action vs autonomous / operator-driven), `is_alertable` (whether the trigger fires a security notification chain), `audit_severity` (info / warn / critical — feeds the compliance report grouping).

impersonation_end_triggers.description: The impersonator hit /end voluntarily. The normal happy path — a support agent finished troubleshooting + closed their session. Fires ImpersonationSessionEnded (queue: notifications-critical), notifies impersonated User via ImpersonationSessionEndedNotification (cannot-opt-out).

EndImpersonationSessionsJob observed the session past ends_at and force-transitioned it. Common — a support agent forgot to /end + the session naturally ran out. Fires ImpersonationSessionEnded (queue: notifications-critical), notifies impersonated User.

A super_admin (or security team member with the terminate ability) fired /end on someone else's session (usually the impersonator's own manager cutting a session short). Also fires when a PlatformUser is offboarded / suspended mid-session — the TerminateActivePlatformImpersonationSessions listener sets this trigger. Fires ImpersonationSessionEnded (NOT ForcedTerminate — that's for security lockdowns).

The security team fired /force-terminate — either directly OR indirectly through the compliance::TenantSecurityIncidentDeclared listener. This is the emergency STOP-EVERYTHING trigger. Fires ImpersonationSessionForcedTerminate (queue: notifications-critical), notifies the impersonated User + the security team via Slack #security + PagerDuty on-call rotation.

notes: Adding a new trigger requires: (1) schema migration to extend the Postgres enum, (2) update this file + regenerate the enum class, (3) update the ImpersonationEndTrigger cast, (4) coordinate with the security team on the routing / notification chain for the new trigger.

audit_severity is deliberately NOT stored on the impersonation_sessions row — the severity is derived from end_trigger at query time so the compliance report can re-classify past sessions if the severity mapping ever changes (rare, but valued for retroactive compliance work).

The is_alertable flag drives which triggers fire security team notifications. Only security_lockdown is alertable today; if we later add a 'suspicious_activity' trigger (e.g. session accessed a resource the operator's abilities didn't cover — should never happen but paranoid audit), that would also be alertable.
